title: evolvulus sericeus subsp. lanuginosus
intro: Evolvulus sericeus subsp. lanuginosus, commonly known as Dwarf Blue Skyflower, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Evolvulus sericeus subsp. lanuginosus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Its therapeutic actions are attributed to its bioactive constituents, which include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, that may help to protect against oxidative stress and modulate immune responses.
benefits: Evolvulus sericeus subsp. lanuginosus has many benefits, such as promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels by releasing calming essential oils, which can improve sleep quality and overall well-being. Its anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, eczema, and other skin conditions, while its antimicrobial properties can aid in wound healing and prevent infections. Additionally, this plant has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. By incorporating Evolvulus sericeus subsp. lanuginosus into one's life, individuals can experience improved mental and physical health, leading to a more balanced and fulfilling lifestyle.
actions: Evolvulus sericeus subsp. lanuginosus has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate conditions like arthritis, wounds, and oxidative stress. Its antispasmodic and expectorant properties also aid in relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, thereby improving breathing and overall lung function. Furthermore, its antihistaminic and anti-allergic properties help alleviate symptoms of allergic reactions, such as itching, redness, and swelling, thereby improving skin health and reducing discomfort. By harnessing these therapeutic actions, people can enjoy improved physical comfort, enhanced respiratory well-being, and reduced symptoms of allergic reactions, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
constituents: Evolvulus sericeus subsp. lanuginosus has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, alkaloids, saponins, and terpenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. These constituents, including compounds like kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin, can help alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress, ultimately enhancing people's quality of life. The plant's saponins, for instance, have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory effects, while its terpenoids have been found to possess anti-cancer properties, providing potential benefits for individuals dealing with chronic illnesses. By harnessing the medicinal potential of Evolvulus sericeus subsp. lanuginosus, researchers aim to develop new treatments and therapies that can improve human health and well-being.
side_effects: Evolvulus sericeus subsp. lanuginosus can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals. Consuming large amounts of the plant's leaves or roots may lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain due to the presence of toxic compounds. In rare cases, excessive consumption of Evolvulus sericeus subsp. lanuginosus has been linked to liver and kidney damage, as well as interactions with certain medications. Additionally, the plant's extracts may exacerbate conditions such as high blood pressure and asthma if used without proper medical supervision.
